[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [30°17′40″N 97°37′16″W﻿ / ﻿30.29444°N 97.62111°W﻿ / 30.29444; -97.62111](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Luedecke_Arena&params=30_17_40_N_97_37_16_W_type:landmark_scale:10000_region:US)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Multi-purpose arena in Austin, Texas

Luedecke Arena

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Map Interactive map of Luedecke Arena | |
| Address | 7311 Decker Lane |
| Location | [Austin, Texas](/wiki/Austin,_Texas "Austin, Texas") |
| Capacity | 6,400 |
| Construction | |
| Built | 1983 |
| Tenants | |
| Star of Texas Fair & Rodeo [Austin Ice Bats](/wiki/Austin_Ice_Bats "Austin Ice Bats") ([WPHL](/wiki/Western_Professional_Hockey_League "Western Professional Hockey League")/[CHL](/wiki/Central_Hockey_League "Central Hockey League")) (1996–2006) [Texas Terminators](/wiki/Texas_Terminators "Texas Terminators") ([IPFL](/wiki/Indoor_Professional_Football_League "Indoor Professional Football League")) (1999) Austin Knights/Rockers ([NIFL](/wiki/National_Indoor_Football_League "National Indoor Football League")) (2002–2003) [Austin Turfcats](/wiki/Austin_Turfcats "Austin Turfcats") ([SIFL](/wiki/Southern_Indoor_Football_League "Southern Indoor Football League")/[IFL](/wiki/Indoor_Football_League "Indoor Football League")) (2009–2010) [Austin Wild](/wiki/Austin_Wild "Austin Wild") ([AAL](/wiki/American_Arena_League "American Arena League")/IAFL) (2018–2019) | |

The **Luedecke Arena** is a 6,400-seat multi-purpose [arena](/wiki/Arena "Arena") on the grounds of the [Travis County](/wiki/Travis_County,_Texas "Travis County, Texas") Exposition Center in [Austin, Texas](/wiki/Austin,_Texas "Austin, Texas").

Built in 1983, the arena is home to the Star of Texas Fair & Rodeo, the [Republic of Texas Biker Rally](/wiki/Republic_of_Texas_Biker_Rally "Republic of Texas Biker Rally") and the Austin Power [indoor soccer](/wiki/Indoor_soccer "Indoor soccer") team. The arena was formerly home to the [Austin Ice Bats](/wiki/Austin_Ice_Bats "Austin Ice Bats") [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") team from 1996 to 2006. It was also home to four [indoor football](/wiki/Indoor_American_football "Indoor American football") teams: the [Texas Terminators](/wiki/Texas_Terminators "Texas Terminators") of the [IPFL](/wiki/Indoor_Professional_Football_League "Indoor Professional Football League") in 1999, the Austin Knights/Rockers in 2002 and 2003, the [Austin Turfcats](/wiki/Austin_Turfcats "Austin Turfcats") in 2009 and 2010, and the [Austin Wild](/wiki/Austin_Wild "Austin Wild") in 2018 and 2019. In 2025, it will supposedly be home to the Austin Wranglers of the [National Gridiron League](/wiki/National_Gridiron_League_(United_States) "National Gridiron League (United States)"). [[1]](#cite_note-1)

Depending on configuration, the arena floor can be configured to seat up to an additional 3,000 for a variety of events.
